faq.sectionSub: Everything you need to know about BG-Zero

q1.question: Is BG-Zero completely free?
q1.answer: Yes, BG-Zero is 100% free to use. There are no watermarks, no file size limits, and no hidden fees. You can remove backgrounds from as many images as you want.

q2.question: Does BG-Zero upload my images to a server?
q2.answer: No. All AI processing runs locally in your browser using WebAssembly technology. Your images never leave your device, ensuring complete privacy and data security.

q3.question: Which AI engines does BG-Zero support?
q3.answer: BG-Zero offers three AI engines: imgly (IS-Net model, ~40MB, compatible with all browsers), Transformers.js (RMBG-1.4, ~176MB, best quality, requires WebGPU), and rembg-web (7+ models from 4.7MB, MIT licensed).

q4.question: Can I use BG-Zero offline?
q4.answer: After the first visit, AI model files are cached in your browser. Subsequent visits can process images even without an internet connection, as long as the model cache hasn't been cleared.

q5.question: What image formats are supported?
q5.answer: BG-Zero supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and BMP formats. The maximum file size is 25MB. Results are exported as transparent PNG files.

q6.question: What's the difference between Auto and Manual mode?
q6.answer: Auto mode uses AI to detect and remove backgrounds with one click — perfect for most images. Manual mode lets you pick specific colors with an eyedropper and use Flood Fill for precise removal, with a mask brush to protect areas you want to keep.

auto_q1.question: How good is the AI auto background removal? Can it handle hair?
auto_q1.answer: BG-Zero's auto mode uses advanced AI models that can accurately handle complex edges like hair and fur. For best results, use the Transformers.js (RMBG-1.4) engine. For portraits, you can also try rembg-web's u2net_human_seg model.

auto_q2.question: Does auto mode support batch background removal?
auto_q2.answer: Yes. You can upload and process multiple images one after another, all locally in your browser with no quantity limits. Each image can be downloaded as a transparent PNG as soon as processing is complete.

auto_q3.question: What's the difference between the three AI engines? How do I choose?
auto_q3.answer: imgly (IS-Net) works in all browsers, ~40MB model, great for quick use. Transformers.js (RMBG-1.4) gives best quality, ~176MB, requires WebGPU. rembg-web offers 7+ models (from 4.7MB) including portrait and anime modes, MIT licensed for commercial use. Start with imgly, switch to Transformers.js for higher quality.

auto_q4.question: Can I add a white or custom background after removing?
auto_q4.answer: The output is a transparent PNG file. You can add any solid background (white, blue, red) or composite onto any scene using any image editor. Perfect for creating e-commerce white-background product photos or ID photo background changes.

manual_q1.question: When should I use manual mode instead of auto?
manual_q1.answer: Manual mode is ideal when auto mode can't precisely identify the background — for example, when the background and subject are similar in color, when you need to keep specific areas, or when you only want to remove part of the background. Manual mode uses a color picker to precisely select target colors, with a mask brush to protect the subject.

manual_q2.question: How do the color picker and Flood Fill work?
manual_q2.answer: Use the Pick tool to click on a background color in your image. Click 'Remove' to execute Flood Fill, which spreads outward from the pick point, turning similarly colored connected pixels (controlled by the tolerance slider) transparent. You can pick and remove multiple times to gradually clean up the background.

manual_q3.question: What is the mask brush for? How do I protect areas?
manual_q3.answer: The Protect Brush lets you mark areas you want to keep before removing the background. Masked pixels won't be affected by Flood Fill, even if their color is similar to the background. Great for protecting subject edges, text, or objects with background-similar colors. Press M to switch to the brush, E for the eraser.

manual_q4.question: What image formats does manual mode support? Any size limits?
manual_q4.answer: Manual mode supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and BMP formats, up to 25MB per image. Results are exported as transparent PNG files. Unlimited undo/redo is supported (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y), so you can experiment freely.

idphoto_q1.question: Is changing ID photo background color free with BG-Zero?
idphoto_q1.answer: Completely free. BG-Zero has no usage limits, no watermarks, and no fees. You can change your ID photo background as many times as you want.

idphoto_q2.question: Will my ID photo be uploaded to a server? Is it safe?
idphoto_q2.answer: Absolutely not. All processing runs locally in your browser — your ID photo never leaves your device. This is the safest approach for photos containing personal identity information.

idphoto_q3.question: What background colors are supported? Can I use custom colors?
idphoto_q3.answer: After removing the background, you get a transparent PNG. You can add white, blue, red, or any custom color in any image editor. Common ID photo colors (white RGB 255,255,255; blue RGB 67,142,219; red RGB 255,0,0) are all easy to apply.

idphoto_q4.question: Will the resolution decrease after changing the background?
idphoto_q4.answer: No. BG-Zero processes and exports at the original resolution with no scaling or compression. The transparent PNG output maintains the same clarity as the original, meeting all requirements for printing and online submission.

ecom_q1.question: Do BG-Zero white background images meet Amazon/marketplace specs?
ecom_q1.answer: Yes. BG-Zero outputs transparent PNG files. You can easily add a pure white background (RGB 255,255,255) to meet Amazon 2000×2000px, eBay, Shopify and other platform requirements. No watermark, no quality loss.

ecom_q2.question: Can I batch process e-commerce product photos?
ecom_q2.answer: Yes. Upload and process multiple product photos one after another with no quantity limits. All processing runs locally in your browser — no paid subscription needed. Perfect for sellers with large product catalogs.

ecom_q3.question: How do I add a white background after export?
ecom_q3.answer: After downloading the transparent PNG, use any image editor (Photoshop, Figma, even phone gallery apps) to add a white layer beneath the transparent one. You can also use online tools to composite white background images.

ecom_q4.question: How does it handle complex products (glass, jewelry)?
ecom_q4.answer: For most products, AI auto mode delivers great results. For transparent/reflective materials (glass bottles, jewelry), use the Transformers.js engine for best quality, or combine with manual mode to refine edges.

compare_q1.question: Which is better, BG-Zero or remove.bg?
compare_q1.answer: Both deliver comparable AI quality, but BG-Zero offers three engines to choose from (imgly, Transformers.js, rembg-web). More importantly, BG-Zero is 100% free with local processing, while remove.bg's free tier is low-resolution only and requires uploading images.

compare_q2.question: Which background remover is best for e-commerce sellers?
compare_q2.answer: BG-Zero is ideal for sellers who need batch processing with privacy. It's completely free, no watermark, no limits, and all product photos are processed locally. remove.bg and iLoveIMG free tiers have resolution or quantity restrictions.

compare_q3.question: Is there a truly free background remover with no watermark?
compare_q3.answer: BG-Zero is completely free with no watermarks. Other tools like remove.bg limit free tier resolution, and iLoveIMG has usage limits. BG-Zero has no hidden fees or feature restrictions.

compare_q4.question: Which tool has the best privacy protection?
compare_q4.answer: BG-Zero offers the best privacy. It processes everything locally in your browser — images never leave your device. remove.bg and iLoveIMG both require uploading images to cloud servers, creating privacy risks for sensitive photos.

video_q1.question: Can I remove video backgrounds for free online?
video_q1.answer: Most online tools currently only support image background removal. Video background removal typically requires professional software (OBS, Premiere) or online services (Unscreen). BG-Zero focuses on images, but you can process video frames with it for similar results.

video_q2.question: What's the difference between video and image background removal?
video_q2.answer: Video background removal is essentially removing backgrounds from every frame, requiring much more computation than a single image. It also needs frame-to-frame consistency (to avoid flickering) and often real-time performance. GPU acceleration is typically required.

video_q3.question: Does BG-Zero support video background removal?
video_q3.answer: BG-Zero currently focuses on image background removal. You can extract video keyframes, batch-remove backgrounds with BG-Zero, and reassemble the video. We're exploring native video background removal features.

video_q4.question: Which tools support real-time video background replacement?
video_q4.answer: OBS Studio (free, open source) supports virtual backgrounds. Zoom/Teams have built-in video background replacement. CapCut offers post-production video cutout. For professional needs, Adobe Premiere's Roto Brush and After Effects deliver the best results.

blogHowToRemoveBg.autoStep4: Click 'Start' and wait for AI processing
blogHowToRemoveBg.autoStep5: Preview the result, download transparent PNG
blogHowToRemoveBg.manualDesc: When auto mode can't achieve perfect results, manual mode offers pixel-level precision. Use the color picker to select background colors, Flood Fill for precise removal, and mask brush to protect the subject from accidental erasure.
blogHowToRemoveBg.manualStep1: Switch to the manual mode page
blogHowToRemoveBg.manualStep2: Upload image, select 'Pick' tool, click the background
blogHowToRemoveBg.manualStep4: Use mask brush to protect subject edges
blogHowToRemoveBg.enginesDesc: BG-Zero offers three AI engines, each with unique strengths:
blogHowToRemoveBg.engineImgly: imgly (IS-Net) — ~40MB, works in all browsers, great for quick use. Good general-purpose results.
blogHowToRemoveBg.engineTransformers: Transformers.js (RMBG-1.4) — ~176MB, requires WebGPU (Chrome 113+). Best quality, especially for complex edges and hair.
blogHowToRemoveBg.engineRembg: rembg-web — From 4.7MB, 7+ models available. Includes portrait-specific (u2net_human_seg), anime-specific (isnet-anime) and more. MIT licensed for commercial use.
blogHowToRemoveBg.scenario1: ID photo background change — remove background, add white/blue/red
blogHowToRemoveBg.scenario2: E-commerce product photos — create clean white background images
blogHowToRemoveBg.scenario3: Social media — create collages and memes with removed backgrounds
blogHowToRemoveBg.scenario4: Presentations — overlay people or objects on slides
blogHowToRemoveBg.scenario5: Graphic design — isolate elements for compositing and creative projects
blogHowToRemoveBg.ctaSub: No signup required, open and use

blogIdPhoto.heading: ID Photo Background Change Tutorial — Free Online in 3 Seconds
blogIdPhoto.heroSub: Step-by-step guide to changing ID photo backgrounds for free. Supports white, blue, and red.
blogIdPhoto.whyTitle: Why Change Your ID Photo Background?
blogIdPhoto.whyDesc: Different scenarios have strict background requirements: job resumes typically need blue or white backgrounds, passport and visa applications require white, exam registrations may need red or blue. Going to a photo studio is time-consuming and expensive — BG-Zero does it in seconds, for free.
blogIdPhoto.specsTitle: ID Photo Background Requirements by Type
blogIdPhoto.spec1: Job Resume / LinkedIn — White or blue background
blogIdPhoto.spec3: UK Passport — Light gray or cream background
blogIdPhoto.spec4: Schengen Visa — White or light gray
blogIdPhoto.stepsTitle: Change ID Photo Background with BG-Zero: Step-by-Step
blogIdPhoto.step1Desc: Visit BG-Zero auto mode page and upload your ID photo. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP formats, up to 25MB.
blogIdPhoto.step2Desc: Select an AI engine (Transformers.js recommended for best portrait quality), click 'Start'. AI completes person detection and background removal in ~5 seconds.
blogIdPhoto.step3Desc: Download the transparent PNG, then add your target background color layer in any image editor (Photoshop, Figma, phone gallery app).
blogIdPhoto.tip1: Wear dark clothing to contrast with the background
blogIdPhoto.tip2: Use Transformers.js engine for best portrait quality
blogIdPhoto.tip3: Switch to manual mode if hair edges need refinement
blogIdPhoto.tip4: Exported transparent PNG preserves full resolution for printing
blogIdPhoto.ctaTitle: Change Your ID Photo Background Now

blogEcommerce.heading: E-commerce Product White Background Tutorial — Free Batch Processing
blogEcommerce.heroSub: Create professional product white background photos with AI, covering all major platform specs.
blogEcommerce.whyTitle: Why Do Marketplaces Require White Backgrounds?
blogEcommerce.whyDesc: Amazon, eBay, Shopify and other marketplaces have clear background requirements for product images. White or transparent backgrounds look more professional and consistent, can increase click-through rates by up to 40%, and reduce returns. Amazon specifically requires main images to have pure white backgrounds (RGB 255,255,255).
blogEcommerce.stepsTitle: Create White Background Product Photos with BG-Zero
blogEcommerce.step1Desc: Visit BG-Zero auto mode, upload your product photo. Shoot in even lighting with clear contrast between product and background for best results.
blogEcommerce.step2Desc: Select imgly engine (fast, compatible), click 'Start'. Preview the result — download when satisfied.
blogEcommerce.step3Desc: Download transparent PNG, add a pure white background layer (RGB 255,255,255) in your editor. For batch processing, repeat with the next image.
blogEcommerce.advanced1: Transparent/glass items — Use Transformers.js engine for better results
blogEcommerce.advanced2: Reflective/metal surfaces — Combine with manual mode to refine reflective edges
blogEcommerce.advanced3: Small accessories/jewelry — Use rembg-web's isnet-general high-precision model
blogEcommerce.advanced4: Multiple products — Photograph and process individually, then composite
blogEcommerce.best1: Export as PNG for highest quality
blogEcommerce.best2: Ensure background is pure white (RGB 255,255,255), not off-white
blogEcommerce.best3: Product should occupy 85%+ of the image area
blogEcommerce.best4: Keep consistent style across all product photos

blogVideo.heading: Video Background Removal Complete Guide — Methods, Tools & Tips (2026)
blogVideo.heroSub: Three methods for video background removal, comparing popular tools with practical tutorials.
blogVideo.whatDesc: Video background removal (video matting) separates the subject from the background in video content. Used in live streaming, short video creation, video conferencing, and e-commerce videos. Unlike image background removal, video requires processing every frame while maintaining frame-to-frame consistency.
blogVideo.methodsTitle: 3 Main Methods for Video Background Removal
blogVideo.method1Desc: OBS Studio (free, open source) and Zoom, Teams have built-in virtual background features. No post-production needed — great for streaming and video calls. Quality depends on camera and lighting; edges may not be perfect.
blogVideo.method2Desc: Adobe Premiere's Roto Brush and CapCut's smart cutout can remove backgrounds during editing. More precise results, but requires editing skills and processing time.
blogVideo.method3Desc: Split video into frame images, batch-remove backgrounds with AI tools, then reassemble. BG-Zero can process video frames — extract keyframes with FFmpeg, remove backgrounds frame by frame with BG-Zero.
blogVideo.bgzeroTitle: Using BG-Zero to Process Video Frames
blogVideo.bgzeroStep1: Extract video keyframes with FFmpeg: ffmpeg -i video.mp4 -vf fps=1 frame_%04d.png
blogVideo.bgzeroStep2: Upload frames to BG-Zero auto mode and remove backgrounds one by one
blogVideo.bgzeroStep4: Reassemble processed frames into video with FFmpeg
blogVideo.bgzeroNote: This method works best for short videos or key scenes that need fine processing. For longer videos, use professional video editing software.
blogVideo.futureDesc: BG-Zero is exploring native video background removal. As browser-based AI technology advances, real-time video background processing in the browser is becoming feasible. Stay tuned for updates.
blogVideo.faqSub: What you need to know about video background removal
blogVideo.ctaSub: BG-Zero currently supports images — video features coming soon

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: BG-Zero ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use our website at bg-zero.online (the "Service").
privacy.imageProcessingTitle: Image Processing — Your Data Stays Local
privacy.imageProcessing: Your images are processed entirely within your browser using WebAssembly and WebGPU technology. We do NOT upload, receive, store, or have any access to your images. No image data ever reaches our servers. This is a core design principle of BG-Zero.
privacy.dataWeCollectIntro: We collect limited personal data necessary to provide the Service:
privacy.dataEmail: Email address — collected during registration or Google OAuth sign-in, used for account management and communication.
privacy.dataPassword: Password hash — stored securely using bcrypt, never in plaintext.
privacy.dataOAuth: Google OAuth profile — name and avatar URL, if you sign in with Google.
privacy.dataSession: Session data — stored in cookies and our database for authentication (7-day expiry).
privacy.dataAnalytics: Analytics events — page views and feature usage, collected for improving the service. No personally identifiable information beyond user ID (for logged-in users).
privacy.dataLogs: Server logs — IP addresses may be temporarily logged for rate limiting and security (retained up to 30 days).
privacy.legalBasis: We process your data under the following legal bases: Contract necessity (Art. 6(1)(b)) for account management and service delivery; Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) for analytics and security.
privacy.thirdPartyIntro: We use the following third-party services:
privacy.thirdPartyHosting: Hosting & CDN — for serving the website globally
privacy.thirdPartyDB: Database provider — for storing account data
privacy.thirdPartyResend: Resend (US) — for sending transactional emails (verification, password reset)
privacy.thirdPartyGoogle: Google OAuth (US) — for social login (only when you choose to sign in with Google)
privacy.thirdPartyHF: Hugging Face CDN (US) — for downloading AI model files directly to your browser (no personal data transmitted)
privacy.retention: Account data is retained for the duration of your account. Analytics events are retained for up to 12 months. Server logs are retained for up to 30 days. You may request deletion of your data at any time.
privacy.yourRightsIntro: Under GDPR, CCPA, and other applicable laws, you have the right to:
privacy.rightAccess: Access — request a copy of your personal data
privacy.rightCorrection: Correction — request correction of inaccurate data
privacy.rightDeletion: Deletion — request deletion of your account and data
privacy.rightPortability: Data portability — export your data in machine-readable format
privacy.rightObjection: Object — object to processing based on legitimate interest
privacy.rightWithdraw: Withdraw consent — where processing is based on consent
privacy.rightsExercise: To exercise these rights, use the account settings page (for deletion and export) or email us at privacy@bg-zero.online.
privacy.children: BG-Zero is not directed at children under 13 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us at privacy@bg-zero.online.
privacy.security: We implement appropriate security measures including: HTTPS encryption, bcrypt password hashing, Content Security Policy headers, rate limiting, and secure session management.
privacy.international: Your data may be processed in the United States through our service providers. We ensure appropriate safeguards (Standard Contractual Clauses) are in place for EU/UK data transfers.
privacy.changes: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify registered users of material changes via email. The "Effective Date" at the top indicates when this policy was last revised.
privacy.contact: For privacy-related inquiries, contact us at: privacy@bg-zero.online
privacy.supervisory: EU/UK residents have the right to lodge a complaint with their local data protection authority if they believe their data has been processed unlawfully.

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.acceptance: By accessing or using BG-Zero (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.
terms.serviceDesc: BG-Zero is a free, open-source AI-powered background removal tool. All image processing runs locally in your web browser. We do not upload, store, or access your images.
terms.noWarranty: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. We do not guarantee accuracy, reliability, or availability of the Service. This is consistent with AGPL-3.0 Sections 15-17.
terms.account: You may create an account to access certain features. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials. You must provide accurate information during registration.
terms.prohibitedIntro: You agree NOT to use the Service to:
terms.prohibited1: Process, create, or distribute child sexual abuse material (CSAM)
terms.prohibited2: Create deepfake content for defamation, fraud, or impersonation
terms.prohibited3: Process copyrighted images for commercial counterfeiting without authorization
terms.prohibited4: Conduct automated mass requests, DDoS attacks, or scraping against our servers
terms.prohibited5: Violate any applicable law or regulation
terms.prohibited6: Circumvent security measures or abuse the Service infrastructure
terms.content: You retain all rights to your images. BG-Zero does not claim any rights over your content — because we never receive it. You are solely responsible for ensuring you have the right to process any images you upload.
terms.ip: The BG-Zero source code is owned by its contributors and licensed under AGPL-3.0. The BG-Zero name, logo, and branding are owned by the project maintainers.
terms.modelLicense: Different AI engines use models with different licenses. The Transformers.js engine uses RMBG-1.4 which is licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial use only). Users are responsible for complying with model licenses. The rembg-web engine uses MIT-licensed models suitable for any use.
terms.termination: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms. You may delete your account at any time through account settings.
terms.limitation: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, BG-Zero and its contributors shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of data or profits, arising from your use of the Service.
terms.indemnity: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless BG-Zero and its contributors from any claims, damages, or expenses arising from your violation of these Terms or misuse of the Service.
terms.governingLaw: These Terms are governed by the laws of Hong Kong SAR, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of Hong Kong.
terms.changes: We may update these Terms from time to time. Continued use of the Service after changes constitutes acceptance. Material changes will be communicated via email to registered users.
terms.contact: For questions about these Terms, contact us at: legal@bg-zero.online
terms.abuse: To report abuse or violations, email: abuse@bg-zero.online

accessibility.heading: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.intro: BG-Zero is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We aim to conform with WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards.
accessibility.status: Partially conformant. We are actively working to improve accessibility across all pages.
accessibility.measure1: Semantic HTML structure with ARIA landmarks
accessibility.measure3: Color contrast ratios meeting WCAG AA standards
accessibility.knownIssues: We are aware of and working on the following accessibility issues. If you encounter additional barriers, please let us know.
accessibility.feedback: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of BG-Zero. Please contact us at: accessibility@bg-zero.online
accessibility.enforcement: If you are not satisfied with our response to your accessibility concern, you may escalate to your local equality body or regulatory authority.

dmca.heading: DMCA Copyright Notice
dmca.intro: BG-Zero processes images entirely within the user's browser. We do not store, host, or transmit user-uploaded images. We are therefore generally not a "service provider" under 17 U.S.C. Section 512 with respect to user-processed images.
dmca.websiteContent: If you believe our website content (not user-processed images) infringes your copyright, please send a DMCA-compliant notice to:
dmca.requirementsIntro: A valid DMCA notice must include:
dmca.req1: Identification of the copyrighted work claimed to be infringed
dmca.req2: Identification of the material that is claimed to be infringing and its location on our site
dmca.req3: Your contact information (name, address, phone, email)
dmca.req4: A statement that you have a good faith belief the use is not authorized
dmca.req5: A statement, under penalty of perjury, that the information is accurate and you are authorized to act on behalf of the copyright owner
dmca.response: We will respond to valid DMCA notices within 48 hours and take appropriate action.

consent.title: A tiny cookie question
consent.description: We'd like to use Google Analytics to understand how people use BG-Zero. No image data is ever sent — images stay 100% in your browser. See our <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.
